title: Establish metrics registry infrastructure with atomic snapshot support
purpose: Bootstrap the metrics subsystem with the module skeleton and the MetricsRegistry type that all metric collectors register into. The registry must support atomic snapshot reads so that Prometheus scrapes see a consistent view across all metric families. This is the leaf dependency for every other metrics brief (OBS-002 through OBS-005).
task: Create the metrics module at crates/liminal/src/metrics/ with a mod.rs that contains only pub mod and pub use declarations. Implement MetricsRegistry in registry.rs as the central coordination point for metric collectors. The registry must support registration of counters, gauges, and histograms by name, and provide an atomic snapshot method that returns a consistent read of all registered metrics. Add pub mod metrics to lib.rs. The registry must be Send + Sync, Clone (handle-style), and impose zero overhead when no exporter is attached.

requirements.title: Create metrics module skeleton
requirements.spec: crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs SHALL declare pub mod for registry (and future sub-modules channel, conversation, pressure, export). mod.rs SHALL contain only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. mod.rs SHALL NOT contain any function, struct, trait, or impl definitions.
acceptance: crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs exists
mod.rs declares pub mod registry
mod.rs contains zero function definitions, zero struct definitions, zero trait definitions
cargo check -p liminal passes

files: crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs:1-1 — currently only a placeholder comment; replace with declarations/re-exports only.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-5 — local example of a compliant module root: `pub mod` declarations followed by `pub use` re-exports.
docs/design/observability/design.json:78-85 — design assigns `metrics/mod.rs` and `metrics/registry.rs` to OBS-001.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-002.json:103-119 — later brief explicitly adds `pub mod channel`, so do not predeclare missing future modules here.
context: `metrics/mod.rs` already exists but is not an acceptable skeleton because it contains only a comment and declares no `registry` module.
CN3 is strict: module roots should contain only `pub mod` declarations and `pub use` re-exports; no structs, functions, impls, tests, or module-local logic.
Declaring `channel`, `conversation`, `pressure`, or `export` now would require files that this brief does not create and would conflict with later OBS briefs that own those modules.
scout.approach: Replace the placeholder with `pub mod registry;` plus `pub use registry::{...};` for the public registry types that this brief defines, especially `MetricsRegistry`. Keep the file tiny and literal: no comments or doc comments if you want to avoid any debate over C1's "only pub mod and pub use" language. Add future module declarations only in their owning briefs, when their files exist.
scout.notes: The parenthetical about future submodules is easy to over-read. In this repo, OBS-002 owns adding `metrics/channel.rs` and `pub mod channel`; predeclaring missing modules in OBS-001 will break `cargo check -p liminal`.

files_changed.note: Replaced placeholder comment with `pub mod registry;` and `pub use` re-exports only.

dev.how: The metrics module root now declares the registry submodule and re-exports the registry API while containing no functions, structs, traits, impls, tests, or module-local logic. Future channel/conversation/pressure/export modules were not predeclared, matching the scout warning that later briefs own those files.

requirements.spec: crates/liminal/src/lib.rs SHALL declare pub mod metrics so the metrics subsystem is part of the public API surface. lib.rs SHALL re-export MetricsRegistry from the metrics module.

crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-5 — shows the crate convention of re-exporting important submodule types from the module root.

Follow the existing style: module declarations first, blank line, then `pub use ...` re-exports.

The root API only needs `pub use metrics::MetricsRegistry;`; additional metric handles can remain available through `liminal::metrics::...` unless the implementation chooses to expose them from `metrics/mod.rs`.

Implement R1/R3 first or in the same patch, because the root re-export will not compile until `metrics/mod.rs` re-exports `MetricsRegistry` from `registry.rs`.
scout.notes: Keep `lib.rs` as an entrypoint: do not put registry logic or global-registry state in the crate root.

requirements.title: Define MetricKind enum for counter, gauge, and histogram
requirements.spec: MetricKind SHALL have exactly three variants: Counter, Gauge, and Histogram. Counter is a monotonically increasing u64. Gauge is a signed i64 that can go up or down. Histogram records individual observations into configurable buckets. MetricKind SHALL derive Debug, Clone.
acceptance: MetricKind::Counter, MetricKind::Gauge, and MetricKind::Histogram are the only variants
MetricKind derives Debug and Clone
MetricKind is defined in crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs

files: crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:new — create this file and define `MetricKind` here.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-001.json:49-56 — exact `MetricKind` variants and derives required.
docs/design/observability/design.json:138-154 — CN2/CN4 require no panics and `Debug` for all public types/errors.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:5-11 — nearby public enum derive style to mirror.
context: `MetricKind` must have exactly three variants: `Counter`, `Gauge`, and `Histogram`; do not encode units, labels, or Prometheus concepts as variants.
The required derives are `Debug` and `Clone`; adding `Copy`, `PartialEq`, and `Eq` is useful for duplicate-kind checks and should not violate the brief.
This enum belongs in `registry.rs`, not in `mod.rs`, because module roots are declaration/re-export only.
scout.approach: Start `registry.rs` with the core public data types and define `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)] pub enum MetricKind { Counter, Gauge, Histogram }`. Keep semantic details in separate handle/snapshot/value types so the enum remains the exact three-variant classifier required by acceptance.
scout.notes: Avoid adding placeholder variants like `Untyped`, `Summary`, or `Unknown`; tests/review can check the variant list directly.

dev.how: `MetricKind` is defined in `crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs` with exactly the required `Counter`, `Gauge`, and `Histogram` variants and derives `Debug` and `Clone` plus comparison/copy traits used by registry validation.

checklist.note: MetricKind is part of the registry infrastructure in registry.rs.

acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:18-23 defines `MetricKind` with only `Counter`, `Gauge`, and `Histogram` variants.

acceptance.criterion: MetricKind derives Debug and Clone
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:18 derives `Debug, Clone` (plus Copy/PartialEq/Eq).

acceptance.criterion: MetricKind is defined in crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs

requirements.title: Define MetricValue types with atomic backing
requirements.spec: Counter SHALL be backed by AtomicU64. Gauge SHALL be backed by AtomicI64. Histogram SHALL store observations in a thread-safe structure with configurable bucket boundaries. All metric values SHALL be readable without locking the writer path. Recording a metric value SHALL NOT block the caller — all updates are atomic or lock-free.
acceptance: Counter uses AtomicU64 with Relaxed ordering for increments
Gauge uses AtomicI64 with Relaxed ordering for set/increment/decrement
Histogram stores observations in a thread-safe bucketed structure
No Mutex or RwLock is held during metric recording

files: crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:new — implement live counter/gauge/histogram storage and recording handles here.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-001.json:66-74 — exact atomic-backing and non-blocking recording acceptance.
docs/design/observability/design.json:155-166 — CN5/CN7: recording must not block actors; snapshots must support consistent export.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace denies unsafe, unwrap/expect, and panic in production code.
context: Counter recording should be a handle method over `AtomicU64::fetch_add(..., Ordering::Relaxed)`; gauge methods should use `AtomicI64` with relaxed `store`/`fetch_add`/`fetch_sub`.

Do not make recording methods look up the metric in a registry map. Registration may use a lock, but the returned handle must hold an `Arc` to the atomic collector so recording is only atomic operations.
Histograms need immutable bucket boundaries plus atomic bucket counters. If you also store totals/sums, use a coherent non-blocking read strategy so snapshots do not see half of one observation.
Because `unsafe_code` is denied workspace-wide, avoid raw-pointer or custom lock-free structures that require unsafe blocks.
scout.approach: Use small internal collector structs such as `CounterMetric { value: AtomicU64 }`, `GaugeMetric { value: AtomicI64 }`, and `HistogramMetric { boundaries: Vec<u64>, counts: Vec<AtomicU64>, overflow: AtomicU64, version: AtomicU64 }`, each wrapped in `Arc` by its typed handle. `CounterHandle::increment`, `GaugeHandle::{set, increment, decrement}`, and `HistogramHandle::observe` should be infallible and should not allocate, lock, or touch the registry map. For histogram snapshots, either avoid separate aggregate fields or use a version counter around multi-atomic updates/loads so a single observation is not represented partially.
scout.notes: The subtle part is histogram consistency: per-bucket atomics are thread-safe, but a snapshot can still observe bucket/count fields from different instants if an observation updates multiple atomics. Prefer one atomic bucket increment per observation, or add a seqlock-style atomic version that never blocks writers.

files_changed.note: Added public metric values, registry-owned collector enum, and snapshot plumbing.
Added AtomicU64 counter, AtomicI64 gauge, atomic bucketed histogram, and non-blocking recording handles.

dev.how: Counters use `AtomicU64::fetch_add(..., Ordering::Relaxed)`, gauges use `AtomicI64` relaxed store/add/sub operations, and histograms use immutable bucket boundaries plus one `AtomicU64` counter per bucket. Returned handles hold `Arc`s directly to collectors, so recording does not acquire registry locks, allocate, or perform map lookup; histogram observations update exactly one atomic bucket counter to avoid partial multi-field observation state.
dev.deviation: The scout placed collector structs in registry.rs; I moved the atomic collector internals and recording handles into a private `registry/collectors.rs` submodule to keep `registry.rs` under the CN1 500-line limit. The public registry/value types remain in registry.rs and behavior follows the scouted design.

checklist.note: The registry owns collector arcs, and recording paths are atomic-only through returned handles.

acceptance.criterion: Counter uses AtomicU64 with Relaxed ordering for increments
acceptance.evidence: Counter storage is `AtomicU64` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:6-15; hardened recording uses `fetch_update(Ordering::Relaxed, Ordering::Relaxed, ...)` with saturating addition at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:93-99. Test `counter_saturates_instead_of_wrapping` passed.

acceptance.criterion: Gauge uses AtomicI64 with Relaxed ordering for set/increment/decrement
acceptance.evidence: Gauge storage is `AtomicI64` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:23-31; `set`, `increment_by`, and `decrement_by` use relaxed atomic store/add/sub at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:113-131.

acceptance.criterion: Histogram stores observations in a thread-safe bucketed structure
acceptance.evidence: Histogram stores immutable boundaries and `Vec<AtomicU64>` bucket counts at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:40-49; observation maps a value to a bucket and atomically increments the bucket at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:145-150.

acceptance.criterion: No Mutex or RwLock is held during metric recording
acceptance.evidence: Recording handle methods at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:88-157 use Arc references and atomic operations only. The registry `RwLock` is used for registration/snapshot state at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:224-240, not in handle recording methods.

issues: Counter recording originally used wrapping `AtomicU64::fetch_add`, so incrementing a counter at `u64::MAX` would wrap to zero and violate the counter's monotonic semantics.
fixes: Changed counter increments to relaxed atomic `fetch_update` with `saturating_add` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:93-99.
Added test `counter_saturates_instead_of_wrapping` at crates/liminal/tests/metrics_registry.rs:18-29; it passed under `cargo test -p liminal`.

requirements.title: Implement MetricsRegistry with registration and lookup
requirements.spec: MetricsRegistry SHALL allow registering named metrics with labels. Registration SHALL return a handle to the metric for recording. MetricsRegistry SHALL be Clone (Arc-wrapped inner), Send + Sync. Duplicate registration of the same name with incompatible MetricKind SHALL return an error. MetricsRegistry SHALL NOT impose any limit on the number of registered metrics.
acceptance: MetricsRegistry::new() creates an empty registry
register_counter(name, labels) returns a CounterHandle
register_gauge(name, labels) returns a GaugeHandle
register_histogram(name, labels, buckets) returns a HistogramHandle
MetricsRegistry is Clone + Send + Sync
Registering the same name with a different MetricKind returns Err

files: crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:new — implement `MetricsRegistry`, typed registration methods, handles, labels, and registration errors here.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-001.json:84-94 — exact registration/duplicate-kind/Clone+Send+Sync acceptance.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-39 — existing public error type derives `Debug` and implements `std::error::Error` via `thiserror`.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:36-60 — local handle pattern: cloneable/debug public handles with infallible constructors where possible.
docs/design/observability/checklist.json:5-9 — C2 names the registry as the collector owner and snapshot provider.
context: `MetricsRegistry` should be handle-style: `#[derive(Clone, Debug)] pub struct MetricsRegistry { inner: Arc<RegistryInner> }`, which gives cheap clones and Send+Sync if the inner storage is Send+Sync.
It is acceptable for registration and snapshot enumeration to use `std::sync::RwLock`/`Mutex`; the R4/R5 hot-path prohibition is about metric recording through returned handles.
Labels should be canonicalized, e.g. sorted `Vec<(String, String)>` or a `BTreeMap`, so the same label set in a different order does not create duplicate metrics.
Duplicate kind checks should be by metric family/name: the same name should not be registered as both counter and gauge across different label sets.
scout.approach: Build an unbounded registry map keyed by `(name, labels)` and a separate `kind_by_name` map to reject incompatible kind reuse. `MetricsRegistry::new()` creates empty maps; `register_counter`, `register_gauge`, and `register_histogram` normalize the labels, validate the name's existing kind, then either return the existing typed handle or insert a new `Arc` collector and return its handle. Define a public `MetricRegistrationError` (or similarly named error) with `Debug`, `Display`, and `std::error::Error`; using the already-present `thiserror` is okay, but a manual implementation avoids touching the manifest.
scout.notes: For histograms, decide how to treat duplicate registration with the same name/labels/kind but different bucket boundaries. Returning `Err` is safer than silently reusing incompatible buckets, even though the acceptance only explicitly mentions different `MetricKind`.

files_changed.note: Implemented MetricsRegistry, registration methods, canonical labels, duplicate-kind validation, and registration errors.
Provided cloneable/debug public CounterHandle, GaugeHandle, and HistogramHandle returned by registration.

dev.how: `MetricsRegistry` is a cloneable `Arc`-backed handle over unbounded `BTreeMap` registry state. It provides `new`, `register_counter`, `register_gauge`, and `register_histogram`, canonicalizes label vectors, reuses compatible existing collectors, rejects name reuse with incompatible `MetricKind`, and rejects duplicate histogram registrations with incompatible buckets. The error type implements `Debug`, `Display`, and `std::error::Error`.
dev.deviation: The implementation uses a private collectors submodule for CN1 file-size compliance; registration logic and ownership remain in registry.rs as scouted.

checklist.note: MetricsRegistry holds all registered collectors and exposes typed registration handles.

stories.note: The registry now supplies the owned metric data model future Prometheus export will scrape from.

acceptance.criterion: MetricsRegistry::new() creates an empty registry
acceptance.evidence: `MetricsRegistry::new()` constructs an `Arc<RegistryInner::new()>` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:116-122; `RegistryInner::new()` initializes default empty registry state at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:297-301.

acceptance.evidence: `register_counter` is defined at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:127-150 and returns `Ok(CounterHandle { metric })` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:146-148.

acceptance.criterion: register_gauge(name, labels) returns a GaugeHandle
acceptance.evidence: `register_gauge` is defined at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:155-178 and returns `Ok(GaugeHandle { metric })` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:174-176.

acceptance.criterion: register_histogram(name, labels, buckets) returns a HistogramHandle
acceptance.evidence: `register_histogram` is defined at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:184-221 and returns `Ok(HistogramHandle { metric })` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:213-214.

acceptance.criterion: MetricsRegistry is Clone + Send + Sync
acceptance.evidence: `MetricsRegistry` derives `Clone, Debug` and wraps `Arc<RegistryInner>` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:111-114; compile-time test `metrics_registry_is_clone_send_sync` at crates/liminal/tests/metrics_registry.rs:4-9 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Registering the same name with a different MetricKind returns Err
acceptance.evidence: `ensure_name_kind` rejects an occupied name whose kind differs at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:416-424; test `duplicate_name_with_different_kind_returns_error` at crates/liminal/tests/metrics_registry.rs:70-83 passed.

requirements.title: Implement atomic snapshot reads
requirements.spec: MetricsRegistry::snapshot() SHALL return a MetricsSnapshot containing a consistent point-in-time view of all registered metrics and their current values. The snapshot SHALL read all metrics atomically so that no partial update is visible. MetricsSnapshot SHALL implement Debug. The snapshot SHALL NOT hold references to the live registry — it is a fully owned copy.
acceptance: MetricsRegistry::snapshot() returns MetricsSnapshot
MetricsSnapshot contains name, labels, kind, and value for every registered metric
MetricsSnapshot implements Debug
MetricsSnapshot is fully owned (no lifetime parameters)
Concurrent writes during snapshot do not cause data races or panics

files: crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:new — define `MetricsSnapshot` and owned metric snapshot/value structs here.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-001.json:103-113 — exact snapshot ownership and contents acceptance.
docs/design/observability/design.json:164-166 — CN7: Prometheus export must render from a consistent registry snapshot.
docs/design/observability/stories.json:8-13 — S5 and related operator stories depend on scrapeable, stable metric data.
context: `MetricsSnapshot` and every nested public snapshot/value type must implement `Debug`; adding `Clone`, `PartialEq`, or `Eq` where practical helps tests and future exporters.
The snapshot must be fully owned: names and labels are cloned into `String`/`Vec` values, and metric values are plain numbers/owned bucket snapshots, not `Arc`, atomics, or references.
A registry read lock can stabilize the set of registered metrics while building the snapshot; recording handles must still continue because they update atomics outside that lock.
HashMap iteration order is nondeterministic; sort snapshot entries or use ordered maps so future Prometheus rendering is stable.
scout.approach: Implement `pub fn snapshot(&self) -> MetricsSnapshot` by enumerating the registered metric entries, cloning each metric's name/labels/kind, and atomically loading its current value into an owned enum such as `MetricValue::Counter(u64)`, `MetricValue::Gauge(i64)`, and `MetricValue::Histogram(HistogramSnapshot)`. If the registry storage is unordered, sort by name and labels before returning. Ensure histogram snapshotting cannot expose a half-updated observation: either keep observations as independent bucket increments or retry around an atomic version counter for multi-field histogram state.
scout.notes: The phrase "consistent point-in-time" is stronger than just "no data races." A Prometheus renderer can be consistent across render time by consuming an owned snapshot, but the registry still needs to avoid internally inconsistent histogram values at snapshot creation.

files_changed.note: Added MetricsSnapshot, MetricSnapshot, MetricValue, HistogramSnapshot, and snapshot reads.
Added atomic collector snapshot helpers used by MetricsRegistry::snapshot().

dev.how: `MetricsRegistry::snapshot()` returns a fully owned `MetricsSnapshot` with deterministic map iteration order from `BTreeMap`, cloning each metric name/label set and atomically loading plain numeric values into owned snapshot structs/enums. Snapshot data contains no lifetimes, atomics, arcs, or live-registry references; concurrent writers use atomics and do not race with snapshot reads.

checklist.note: The registry provides owned atomic snapshot reads across all registered collectors.

stories.note: The owned snapshot is the stable scrape input needed by the future Prometheus-compatible exporter.

acceptance.criterion: MetricsRegistry::snapshot() returns MetricsSnapshot
acceptance.evidence: `MetricsRegistry::snapshot()` is defined at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:223-230 and returns `MetricsSnapshot { metrics }`.

acceptance.criterion: MetricsSnapshot contains name, labels, kind, and value for every registered metric
acceptance.evidence: `MetricSnapshot` owns `name`, `labels`, `kind`, and `value` fields at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:85-90; `MetricEntry::snapshot` populates those fields for every map entry at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:346-352. Test `snapshot_contains_owned_metric_values` at crates/liminal/tests/metrics_registry.rs:85-117 passed.

acceptance.criterion: MetricsSnapshot implements Debug
acceptance.evidence: `MetricsSnapshot` derives `Debug` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:73-76; compile-time test `metrics_snapshot_is_owned_static_debug` at crates/liminal/tests/metrics_registry.rs:11-16 passed.

acceptance.criterion: MetricsSnapshot is fully owned (no lifetime parameters)
acceptance.evidence: `MetricsSnapshot` stores `Vec<MetricSnapshot>` with no lifetime parameters at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:73-76; `MetricSnapshot` uses owned `String`, `Vec`, `MetricKind`, and `MetricValue` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:85-98. The `'static` compile-time test at crates/liminal/tests/metrics_registry.rs:11-16 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Concurrent writes during snapshot do not cause data races or panics
acceptance.evidence: Test `snapshot_tolerates_concurrent_writes` spawns writer threads while repeatedly calling `registry.snapshot()` at crates/liminal/tests/metrics_registry.rs:32-68; it passed under `cargo test -p liminal`.

requirements.title: Zero overhead when no exporter is attached
requirements.spec: When no MetricsRegistry is installed, all metric recording operations SHALL compile to no-ops or return immediately without performing any work. The system SHALL support a global optional registry that metric sites check before recording. The check SHALL be a single atomic load, not a lock acquisition.
acceptance: A global Option<MetricsRegistry> or AtomicPtr is provided for metric sites
When the global registry is None, recording is a no-op (returns immediately)
The check is a single atomic load, not a Mutex::lock
No allocation occurs when metrics are disabled

files: crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:new — add safe global optional registry infrastructure here.
docs/design/observability/design.json:21-23 — P4 requires zero overhead when observability is unused.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-001.json:123-131 — exact global optional registry and single-load/no-allocation acceptance.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace lint denies unsafe code, making raw `AtomicPtr` dereferencing a poor fit.
context: A literal raw `AtomicPtr` solution would require unsafe dereferencing and lifetime management; this conflicts with the workspace `unsafe_code = "deny"` lint.
A safe std-only pattern is `OnceLock<MetricsRegistry>` for one-time install plus `AtomicBool` as the disabled fast-path gate.

The disabled path must avoid cloning an `Arc`, allocating labels, or doing registry lookup; future instrumentation sites should first perform a relaxed atomic enabled check and return immediately when false.
Make global installation one-way unless a later brief explicitly needs teardown; one-way install avoids dangling global references.
scout.approach: Add `static METRICS_ENABLED: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);` and `static GLOBAL_METRICS: OnceLock<MetricsRegistry> = OnceLock::new();`. Provide functions like `install_global_registry(registry) -> Result<(), MetricRegistrationError>`, `metrics_enabled() -> bool`, and `global_registry() -> Option<&'static MetricsRegistry>`, where `global_registry` first checks `METRICS_ENABLED.load(Ordering::Relaxed)` and returns `None` immediately when disabled. Set the `OnceLock` before flipping the enabled flag so readers never see enabled-without-registry.
scout.notes: If review focuses on the phrase "global Option<MetricsRegistry>", frame `OnceLock<MetricsRegistry>` as the safe std optional global under this repo's no-unsafe policy, with the separate `AtomicBool` providing the requested single-load fast path.

files_changed.note: Added optional global registry support using OnceLock plus a relaxed AtomicBool fast-path gate.

dev.how: The module now provides `install_global_registry`, `metrics_enabled`, and `global_registry`. Disabled instrumentation sites can call `metrics_enabled()` for a single relaxed atomic load and return before allocating labels or touching registry state; `global_registry()` also returns immediately on the disabled path without acquiring a lock. Installation stores the registry before flipping the enabled flag so enabled readers never see a missing registry.
dev.deviation: The scout recommended `OnceLock<MetricsRegistry>` plus `AtomicBool` because unsafe raw AtomicPtr dereferencing is not allowed; I followed that safe approach rather than a literal raw AtomicPtr/global mutable Option design.

checklist.note: The optional global registry fast path is implemented in the registry infrastructure without unsafe code or locks on the disabled check.

acceptance.criterion: A global Option<MetricsRegistry> or AtomicPtr is provided for metric sites
acceptance.evidence: The global registry path is implemented with `static METRICS_ENABLED: AtomicBool` and `static GLOBAL_METRICS: OnceLock<MetricsRegistry>` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:15-16, exposed by `global_registry()` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:283-290.

acceptance.criterion: When the global registry is None, recording is a no-op (returns immediately)
acceptance.evidence: `METRICS_ENABLED` starts false at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:15; `global_registry()` returns `None` immediately on the disabled branch at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:283-290, enabling metric sites to return before allocating labels or recording.

acceptance.criterion: The check is a single atomic load, not a Mutex::lock
acceptance.evidence: `metrics_enabled()` performs one atomic load at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:278-280. No Mutex is used anywhere in metrics source; the registry RwLock is for registration/snapshot state, not the disabled fast-path.

acceptance.criterion: No allocation occurs when metrics are disabled
acceptance.evidence: The disabled fast path is `metrics_enabled()`/`global_registry()` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:278-290: one atomic load and a branch returning `None`; no label normalization or map allocation occurs on that path.

issues: The global enabled flag originally used relaxed store/load while the dev report claimed installation stored the registry before enabling readers. Without a release/acquire edge, a reader could observe the enabled flag without synchronized publication of the `OnceLock` contents on weak memory.
fixes: Changed the global enabled flag publication to `Ordering::Release` in `install_global_registry` and the fast-path read to `Ordering::Acquire` at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:268-280, while preserving the single-atomic-load disabled check.

requirements.title: Add metrics dependencies to Cargo.toml
requirements.spec: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml SHALL NOT add any external metrics library (no prometheus crate, no metrics crate). The metrics types are hand-rolled using std::sync::atomic. Cargo.toml SHALL NOT add any new dependencies for this brief — std atomics are sufficient.
acceptance: No new dependencies added to Cargo.toml for metrics primitives
AtomicU64 and AtomicI64 from std::sync::atomic are used directly
cargo check -p liminal passes

files: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-13 — current dependencies are only `beamr` and `thiserror`; no metrics dependency is present.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-001.json:141-148 — exact no-new-dependencies acceptance.
docs/design/observability/design.json:24-26 — Prometheus is an export format, not the runtime metrics model.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace lints already enforce no unsafe/panic/unwrap patterns without adding tooling.
context: Everything needed for this brief is in `std`: `Arc`, `RwLock`/`Mutex` for registration if needed, `OnceLock`, and `std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicI64, AtomicU64, Ordering}`.
Do not add `prometheus`, `metrics`, `opentelemetry`, `parking_lot`, `dashmap`, `arc-swap`, or any telemetry/concurrency helper crate.
The existing `thiserror` dependency can support an error enum if desired, but R8 is best satisfied by leaving `crates/liminal/Cargo.toml` and `Cargo.lock` unchanged.
scout.approach: Make no manifest change for OBS-001. Implement all primitives directly in `registry.rs` using imports from `std::sync` and `std::sync::atomic`; if an error type is needed, either use the already-present `thiserror` or manually implement `Display`/`Error`, but do not edit dependency lists.
scout.notes: The brief metadata says `Cargo.toml` is a modify target, but the spec says not to add dependencies. Treat that as a negative requirement: verify there is no source diff in `crates/liminal/Cargo.toml` or `Cargo.lock` after implementation.

dev.how: No manifest or lockfile dependency changes were made. The implementation uses only standard library synchronization and atomic primitives (`Arc`, `RwLock`, `OnceLock`, `AtomicBool`, `AtomicU64`, `AtomicI64`, and relaxed `Ordering`) plus the crate's pre-existing error/display facilities.

checklist.note: No external metrics/telemetry dependency was added; Cargo.toml and Cargo.lock remain unchanged for this brief.

acceptance.criterion: No new dependencies added to Cargo.toml for metrics primitives
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-13 still lists only existing dependencies `beamr` and `thiserror`; no metrics/prometheus crate was added.

acceptance.criterion: AtomicU64 and AtomicI64 from std::sync::atomic are used directly
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:1-2 imports `AtomicI64`, `AtomicU64`, and `Ordering` from `std::sync::atomic`; counter and gauge storage use them directly at crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:6-15 and 23-31.

acceptance.criterion: cargo check -p liminal passes
acceptance.evidence: `cargo check -p liminal` executed after hardening and exited 0 (`Finished dev profile`).

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement channel-specific metrics — that is OBS-002.
SHALL NOT implement conversation-specific metrics — that is OBS-003.
SHALL NOT implement pressure-specific metrics — that is OBS-004.
SHALL NOT implement Prometheus text rendering — that is OBS-005.
SHALL NOT add any external metrics or telemetry crate dependency.
SHALL NOT implement trace context or structured logging — those are OBS-006 and OBS-007.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
metrics/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic.
Metric recording does not acquire any Mutex or RwLock.
MetricsSnapshot is fully owned with no lifetime parameters.
